response: We propose a new method for [task] and evaluate its performance against a strong baseline on the [dataset] test set. Across five random seeds, our approach achieves a mean accuracy of 73.4 ± 1.1%, compared to 72.2 ± 0.9% for the baseline, corresponding to an absolute improvement of +1.2 percentage points. A two-sample t-test indicates that this difference is not statistically significant (t = 1.04, p = 0.31), with a 95% confidence interval on the gain of [–1.1, +3.5]. These results suggest that while our method offers a modest numerical improvement, the evidence does not support a reliable advantage over the baseline under the current experimental conditions. Our analysis highlights the importance of careful statistical evaluation when benchmarking incremental advances in [task].
